chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI C.P No.S-462 of 2025 Date Order with signature of the Judge Hearing/Priority 1. For hearing of CMA N. 4022 of 2025 (Stay) 2. For hearing of main case 05.06.2025 Mr. Qamar Hussain Advocate for the Petitioner along with Petitioner Uzair Saleem, respondent No.1 present in person ************* Heard learned counsel for the Petitioner at length and the respondent present in person. After hearing the parties, it transpires that directions were issued for the payment of maintenance amount at the rate of Rs.2,50,000/= since June, 2024, however; the respondent has stopped depositing the maintenance since February, 2025 and till date; a sum of Rs.8,80,000/= has become due. The respondent states that due to some losses and bad health, he was unable to deposit the said amount. Secondly, with respect to the passports of the minors which are admittedly lying with the respondent, the respondent undertakes to deposit the same before the trial Court during course of the day in Guardian and Ward Case No. 1238 of 2024. With respect to the impugned order dated 26.05.2025 whereby the custody of all the three minors was ordered to be handed over to the respondent for one month from 04.06.2025 till 06.07.2025. Both the parties state, without prejudice to their rights and contentions, that they have no objection if the custody of two minors namely Muhammad Hamdan Uzair and Baby Haya Uzair both aged around ten years is temporarily handed over to the respondent for one week from 08.06.2025 till 14.06.2025. With respect to the third minor, baby Haya Uzair, who is a female and is of five years of the age, both parties agree by consent that the regular meeting on every Saturday and Sunday, which were already ordered by the trial Court would continue.

Both the parties state, without prejudice to their rights and contentions, that they have no objection if the custody of two minors namely Muhammad Hamdan Uzair and Baby Haya Uzair both aged around ten years is temporarily handed over to the respondent for one week from 08.06.2025 till 14.06.2025. With respect to the third minor, baby Haya Uzair, who is a female and is of five years of the age, both parties agree by consent that the regular meeting on every Saturday and Sunday, which were already ordered by the trial Court would continue. The respondent further undertakes, without prejudice to the rights and contentions of both the parties, that he will directly pay the outstanding School Fees of all the three minors in their respective Schools, as already ordered by the learned Guardian Court, within ten days’ from today all will agitate with respect to the maintenance amounts before Guardian Court. The impugned order dated 26.05.2025 therefore stands modified in the above terms, which has been recorded by consent of both the parties. The instant Constitution Petition therefore stands disposed of in the above terms along with listed application. J U D G E brohips
